name: Garlic-Broccoli Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Place unpeeled garlic cloves in a pot of boiling water for 1 minute (30 seconds for small cloves). Remove from water, peel, and mince.
2. Cut broccoli into florets and stems, discarding any woody parts.
3. Cook the broccoli in boiling water until tender, then remove and drain.
4. Melt butter in a 2-quart saucepan over medium heat.
5. When the butter starts to bubble, add minced garlic and stir rapidly for a few seconds.
6. Quickly add flour, salt, and pepper, stirring constantly for about 1 minute.
7. Gradually add milk and broth (or water) to the mixture, stirring briskly with a wire whisk until the sauce thickens.
8. In a blender or food processor, puree some of the broccoli along with a small amount of the sauce. Then add the remaining sauce in batches, blending continuously until all the broccoli is fully incorporated.
9. Adjust seasonings as needed and thin the soup with half-and-half to reach the desired consistency.
10. Serve sprinkled with Hungarian sweet paprika.
NOTE: This rich and creamy soup develops even better flavor the next day and can be served hot or cold. Add a little extra half-and-half just before serving on the second day.
